title: eryngium nudicaule var. bellidifolium
intro: Eryngium nudicaule var. bellidifolium, also known as Sea Holly or Sea Fennel, is a flowering plant that is a variation of the Sea Holly species, characterized by its compact growth and bell-shaped leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Eryngium nudicaule var. bellidifolium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional.
